assertions.title: Mint race condition fixed

steps.observed: Auth cookie obtained, role=ADMIN, brandId=cmmaoafm700000k14rw6gdmth

steps.action: Create DRAFT product for mint testing
steps.observed: Product cmmbs985f000c8y14ids58610 created with status=DRAFT (201)
Both returned 403 'Insufficient permissions'
403 Forbidden - requireRole('BRAND_ADMIN') rejects ADMIN role

steps.action: Verify auth session is valid via GET /auth/me
steps.observed: Valid session confirmed: role=ADMIN, brandId=cmmaoafm700000k14rw6gdmth

assertions.issues: Mint route uses requireRole('BRAND_ADMIN') which does NOT include ADMIN role. This is inconsistent with other product routes (create uses requireRole('BRAND_ADMIN', 'OPERATOR', 'ADMIN')). The race condition logic (Prisma $transaction with conditional updateMany) exists in code but cannot be tested via API because the ADMIN account is rejected at the RBAC middleware before reaching the handler. The brandId null guard inside the handler also handles ADMIN specially (user.role !== 'ADMIN'), confirming ADMIN was intended to be allowed.

steps.action: POST /products with normal JSON body (Content-Type: application/json)
steps.observed: Product cmmbsbjby000g8y14ha18xrxk created (201) - default JSON parser works

steps.expected: 413 Payload Too Large (default Fastify bodyLimit enforced)
steps.observed: 413 'Request body is too large' - FST_ERR_CTP_BODY_TOO_LARGE
Zero matches - no addContentTypeParser anywhere in api/src/

steps.expected: Mint takes no body, no custom parser
steps.observed: Mint route has no body schema, no content-type parser - uses default Fastify handling
Auth cookie obtained, role=VIEWER, brandId=null
403 'User must belong to a brand' - brandId null guard
403 'Insufficient permissions' - RBAC rejects VIEWER before null guard
403 'User must belong to a brand' - brandId null guard
403 'Insufficient permissions' - RBAC rejects VIEWER before null guard
403 'Insufficient permissions' - RBAC rejects VIEWER before null guard
403 'User must belong to a brand' - brandId null guard

evidence.network: All 6 product endpoints return 403 for null-brandId VIEWER user: GET /products -> 403, POST /products -> 403, GET /products/:id -> 403, PATCH /products/:id -> 403, POST /products/:id/mint -> 403, GET /products/:id/qr -> 403

steps.observed: 400 'Name must be at most 255 characters'
400 'Category must be one of: Leather Goods, Jewelry, Watches, Fashion, Accessories, Other'
400 'Serial number must be at most 100 characters'
400 'Description must be at most 2000 characters'

assertions.title: ADMIN create with explicit brandId

steps.action: POST /products as ADMIN with brandId in body
steps.observed: 201 - product cmmbscl7l000j8y14un3v7rxq created with brandId=cmmaoafm700000k14rw6gdmth
400 'ADMIN must provide brandId in request body'

steps.action: POST /products as ADMIN with second brandId (test-brand-2-id)
steps.observed: 201 - product cmmbscl8r000m8y14gm561drk created with brandId=test-brand-2-id

evidence.network: POST /products (ADMIN+brandId) -> 201, POST /products (ADMIN no brandId) -> 400, POST /products (ADMIN+alt brandId) -> 201

assertions.issues: Could not test non-ADMIN body.brandId ignored behavior - no BRAND_ADMIN account available (only ADMIN and VIEWER accounts provided). The ADMIN-specific behavior (require brandId, allow cross-brand) is fully verified.

frictions.description: Mint route uses requireRole('BRAND_ADMIN') which does not include 'ADMIN' role, unlike other product routes (create, update) which use requireRole('BRAND_ADMIN', 'OPERATOR', 'ADMIN'). This prevented testing C1 race condition since only an ADMIN account was provided.

For C4 testing, some routes (POST, PATCH, mint) return 403 via RBAC check (VIEWER role rejected) before the brandId null guard is reached. The end result is correct (403 on all routes) but the error message differs: 'Insufficient permissions' vs 'User must belong to a brand'.
frictions.resolution: Both paths result in 403 as specified. The RBAC check provides defense-in-depth for routes requiring elevated roles.

frictions.description: No BRAND_ADMIN account was provided for testing, which limits F5 non-ADMIN brandId-ignored behavior and C1 mint race condition testing.

blockers.description: VAL-SEC-C1 cannot be tested via API: mint route's requireRole('BRAND_ADMIN') rejects the ADMIN account (role=ADMIN). No BRAND_ADMIN account was provided. The transaction-based race condition fix exists in code (Prisma $transaction with conditional updateMany) but cannot be exercised through the API with available credentials.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified auth session is valid (GET /auth/me -> 200), confirmed ADMIN role can access other product endpoints (GET /products -> 200, POST /products -> 201). Issue is specific to mint route RBAC configuration.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 4 passed (C2, C4, F4, F5), 1 blocked (C1 - mint route rejects ADMIN role, preventing race condition test). All validation bounds enforce correctly. brandId null guard blocks all product routes for null-brandId users. ADMIN brandId requirement works as specified. Content-type parser is properly scoped (no global override, default bodyLimit enforced).
